perturbation.original_text: 2. Scope of the Joint Venture Business. The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products, (the "Products") that include the display of licensed logos, images, brand names and other labels that differentiate them from the branding (the "PVSS Products") under which PVSS and/or its affiliates, sell to retailers and distributors in the normal course of their business. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall include: (a) The purchase of Products for resale; (b) The acquisition of a license(s) permitting the use of selected images in the Products; (c) The sale and distribution of the Products to retailers and distributors; and, (d) The transaction of such other and further business as is necessary, advisable, or incidental to the business of the Joint Venture. (e) Develop a global marketing program for licensed Products Exhibit A attached hereto, describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Scope of the Joint Venture Business. The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall include: (a) The purchase of products for resale; (b) The acquisition of a license(s) permitting the use of selected images in the products; (c) The sale and distribution of the products to retailers and distributors; and, (d) The transaction of such other and further business as is necessary, advisable, or incidental to the business of the Joint Venture. (e) Develop a global marketing program for licensed products Exhibit A attached hereto, describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Products' and 'PVSS Products' has been removed. This creates an ambiguity regarding the source and nature of the products being marketed. Specifically, the removal of 'that include the display of licensed logos, images, brand names and other labels that differentiate them from the branding (the "PVSS Products") under which PVSS and/or its affiliates, sell to retailers and distributors in the normal course of their business' contradicts the rest of the contract by making the reader think that the JV are marketing batteries unrelated to PVSS, where as in Exhibit A it says Pivotal Self Service Tech, Inc. will 1) Provide the Products in accordance with the specifications and quantities and time frames designated by CCGI, thus causing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pivotal Self Service Tech, Inc. will: 1) Provide the Products in accordance with the specifications and quantities and time frames designated by CCGI 2) Provision any additional Products deemed by the Joint Venturers to be salable through the channels established by CCGI 3) Negotiate such favorable pricing and terms with the suppliers of the Products so as to assure the viability of the Joint Venture offerings and the continuity of Product availability to the customers of the Joint Venture 4) Provide alternate fulfillment and distribution services of the Products as backup to those provided by CCGI
perturbation.justification: The removal of the definition of 'Products' and 'PVSS Products' introduces ambiguity. The changed text no longer specifies that the products marketed by the joint venture are differentiated from those sold by PVSS. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that 'Pivotal Self Service Tech, Inc. will: 1) Provide the Products...' Without the original definition, it's unclear what 'Products' refers to in the 'Contradicted Text,' creating a strong contradiction and uncertainty about the source and nature of the products being marketed.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Powers and Authority of the Joint Venturers. The Joint Venturers shall have full and complete charge of all affairs of the Joint Venture. The Joint Venturers recognize that both of the Joint Venturers are and will continue to be engaged in the conduct of their respective businesses for their own account. Neither Joint Venturer shall be entitled to compensation for services rendered to the Joint Venture as such, but each Joint Venturer shall be reimbursed for all direct expenses, including travel, office, and all other out-of-pocket expenses incurred in the operation of the affairs of the Joint Venture and the promotion of its businesses. It is agreed that either Joint Venturer shall, except as provided for below, have authority to execute instruments of any character relating to the affairs of the Joint Venture; provided, that without the written consent or approval of both of the Joint Venturers: (i) the Joint Venture shall incur no liability of any sort, nor any indebtedness for borrowed funds; (ii) no assets owned in the name of the Joint Venture be disposed of; and (iii) no commitment to purchase any item for the Joint Venture shall be made.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Powers and Authority of the Joint Venturers. The Joint Venturers shall have full and complete charge of all affairs of the Joint Venture. The Joint Venturers recognize that both of the Joint Venturers are and will continue to be engaged in the conduct of their respective businesses for their own account. Neither Joint Venturer shall be entitled to compensation for services rendered to the Joint Venture as such, but each Joint Venturer shall be reimbursed for all direct expenses, including travel, office, and all other out-of-pocket expenses incurred in the operation of the affairs of the Joint Venture and the promotion of its businesses. It is agreed that either Joint Venturer shall have authority to execute instruments of any character relating to the affairs of the Joint Venture; provided, that without the written consent or approval of both of the Joint Venturers: (i) the Joint Venture shall incur no liability of any sort, nor any indebtedness for borrowed funds; (ii) no assets owned in the name of the Joint Venture be disposed of; and (iii) no commitment to purchase any item for the Joint Venture shall be made.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'except as provided for below' creates an internal contradiction, because of the authority to execute intruments, implies that the venturers always have this power, which is in conflict with the restrictions that follow in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Powers and Authority of the Joint Venturers. The Joint Venturers shall have full and complete charge of all affairs of the Joint Venture. The Joint Venturers recognize that both of the Joint Venturers are and will continue to be engaged in the conduct of their respective businesses for their own account. Neither Joint Venturer shall be entitled to compensation for services rendered to the Joint Venture as such, but each Joint Venturer shall be reimbursed for all direct expenses, including travel, office, and all other out-of-pocket expenses incurred in the operation of the affairs of the Joint Venture and the promotion of its businesses. It is agreed that either Joint Venturer shall have authority to execute instruments of any character relating to the affairs of the Joint Venture; provided, that without the written consent or approval of both of the Joint Venturers: (i) the Joint Venture shall incur no liability of any sort, nor any indebtedness for borrowed funds; (ii) no assets owned in the name of the Joint Venture be disposed of; and (iii) no commitment to purchase any item for the Joint Venture shall be made.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "except as provided for below" in the Changed Text creates a direct conflict within Section 5 itself (which is also the Contradicted Text). Originally, the sentence "either Joint Venturer shall, except as provided for below, have authority to execute instruments" acknowledges limitations. Removing this phrase implies unconditional authority, directly contradicting the subsequent clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) that restrict that authority by requiring written consent. This creates a strong, undeniable in-text contradiction according to the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Exhibit A attached hereto, describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.changed_text: Exhibit A describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'attached hereto' has been removed. This creates an internal contradiction if Exhibit A goes missing from the original document, since the exhibit is mentioned in section 2, but it not longer is attached. Which means that section 2 references an exhibit that does not exist. Section 2 says: (e) Develop a global marketing program for licensed Products Exhibit A attached hereto, describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Scope of the Joint Venture Business. The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall include: (a) The purchase of products for resale; (b) The acquisition of a license(s) permitting the use of selected images in the products; (c) The sale and distribution of the products to retailers and distributors; and, (d) The transaction of such other and further business as is necessary, advisable, or incidental to the business of the Joint Venture. (e) Develop a global marketing program for licensed products Exhibit A describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.justification: The removal of "attached hereto" creates a direct contradiction because section 2 explicitly refers to 'Exhibit A'. By removing the attachment reference, the document now references a non-existent exhibit, creating ambiguity and uncertainty. This contradicts the initial statement of having the exhibit attached and makes the responsibilities described in Exhibit A unclear and potentially unenforceable as it is not physically connected to the contract.
